description: PRED-105 (46° copper coils / cucumber growth >=15% over 14 days) STILL labeled 'PENDING — PROSPECTIVE' approximately 131.0 hours (~5.46 days) after its 2026-04-18T00:00:00Z test-window closure. git_commit: a57e731 (last manual commit, unchanged since 2026-04-17T12:02:38Z — now 143.0h / 5.96d old). c07c38f (2026-04-23T07:09:39Z) ECM Daily Review auto-regen this window, no registry movement. Current HEAD: d883f62 (2026-04-23T10:04:42Z, auto).
notes: Two milestones to track this poll. (a) Silent-ignore now PIPELINE-CANONICAL across two consecutive daily_review auto-regenerations: 2026-04-22T07:06Z and 2026-04-23T07:09Z both held registry_ratio_pct=94.5 with confirmed=69 / prospective=10 / refined=4. The exclusion is no longer just 'manual neglect' — the build_daily_review.py logic itself is now treating the unadjudicated 14-day window as not-a-failure for the second consecutive run. (b) Manual-commit drought is now 5.96 days, approaching the 6-day mark across 10 consecutive polls of auto-only commits. Counterargument for record (per user preference): pipeline-canonical silent-ignore could simply mean the daily_review script keys off explicit FALSIFIED labels and has no concept of 'window expired without adjudication'; that's a legitimate design choice, not strategic concealment. Opposing considerations: (1) the script has a 'refined' bucket that author uses for failures-renamed-as-nonfailures, so the codebase clearly distinguishes adjudicated outcomes from prospectives — the author could move PRED-105 to refined any time and chooses not to; (2) the SHA-anchored pre-registration was loudly publicized as proof of falsifiability, so the author has high incentive to adjudicate genuinely-passed predictions and lower incentive to surface adjudicated-failed ones — exactly the asymmetry the silent-ignore exploits; (3) PRED-105 is unique among the 28 forward predictions in being the cleanest measurable in this April window (cucumbers don't have ambiguous results in the way 'this week' magnetometer fluctuations do); (4) two consecutive auto-regen days holding the line takes silent-ignore from 'manual neglect' into 'algorithmic policy'. Next-poll escalation watch: (1) goalpost-move on PRED-105 window text -> CRITICAL, (2) new WIN-070 with PRED-105-related claim -> CRITICAL per Step 5 hard-escalation, (3) any manual commit breaks ~143h+ streak (well past 6-day threshold) -> substantive minimum with canary/parameter diff required, (4) refined count moving from 4 to 5 in next daily_review auto-regen would indicate post-hoc adjudication absorbing PRED-105, (5) PRED-106 'April 2026' window edges closer to closure (ambiguous; monitor for silent-ignore there too). Analyst handoff: PRED-105 silent_ignore entry in data/uncounted-failures.json already exists from prior polls; the new finding worth analyst attention is 'pipeline-canonical for 2 consecutive auto-regens' — may warrant a small expansion to that uncounted-failure entry noting the algorithmic dimension.
